mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals supply-side capacity utilization improvements that expand profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns specifically during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 3.0 percent forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which lifts earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor demand, which supports aggregate consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising employment periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with stable consumer prices indicates benign supply-side expansion that boosts corporate margins without triggering monetary tightening, driving positive equity returns during disinflationary growth phases.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and stable CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observed instances.
